learning_outcomes.description: Identify some of the myths about how the criminal justice system works from a psychological perspective
Understand some of the empirical evidence that can inform our understanding of criminal justice and how justice is administered

assessments.due_date:
3/08/2026 - 21/09/2026

Quizzes can only be attempted and submitted in the workshop.
assessments.task_description:
Every week in the workshop, you’ll take a quiz using traditional bubble sheets and pencils to assess your understanding of the material covered. These weekly quizzes are a crucial part of enhancing your learning and improving retention of the course material. Research indicates that regular testing significantly boosts long-term memory retention compared to merely studying the information (Roediger, Agarwal, McDaniel, McDermott, 2014). These quizzes will challenge you to recall and apply what you’ve learned, helping cement these concepts in your memory.

 

Please note:

  • Quizzes can only be attempted in-class each week. 
  • The quizzes will commence no earlier than 5 minutes after the scheduled time for the start of the class. You must be in class during the testing period (that is, the time from which the test commences until when it concludes) to be eligible to take the test. If you arrive late but before the end of the testing period you will be able to complete the test in the time remaining in the testing period (e.g., if the test begins at 5 minutes past the hour and you arrive at 10 minutes past the hour then you will have 5 minutes to complete the test).
  • To take the quiz you will need to produce a valid UQ student ID card and have a pencil. If you wish, you can bring a paper form dictionary with no annotation. This may be an English or dual language dictionary as required. No additional written, printed, or electronic material will be permitted. For the duration of the test, your mobile phones/smart watch must be turned off or in airplane mode and all other items must be placed on floor under your chair.
  • In the absence of extenuating circumstances (such as becoming ill), if you complete the quiz but then subsequently leave the class before the end of the workshop, then your quiz will not be graded. 
  • There will be 10 questions in each quiz
  • From Episode 2 (Week 3) onwards, quizzes will comprise questions relating to the current week’s content and content from up to 2 weeks prior (e.g., the Episode 3 quiz in Week 4 may include questions related to Episode 1 and Episode 2 as well as Episode 3). In each week the majority of the questions will be on content from that week’s episode.
  • There will be 8 quizzes.
  • Your best 6 marks out of the 8 quizzes will count to your grade.
  • Each quiz is worth 10 marks, and the quizzes contribute 40% of your overall percentage.
  • You must attempt each quiz on your own.
  • You must attempt the quiz for either Episode 7 or Episode 8 of the course in order to be awarded more than a Grade of 4 in the course. An attempt is defined as providing an answer to at least 50% of the multiple choice questions.
  • If you are unwell and unable to complete a quiz, you will only need to provide medical documentation in the event that you miss three or more quizzes. If you miss one or two quizzes, we will simply count your best six attempts at the other quizzes. If you miss a quiz however, make sure you get the appropriate documentation at the time you miss the quiz however, just in case you end up missing more than two and you need to provide it.
  • Important:  You must write your name/student number on your answer sheet for us to be able to give you the marks for this assessment item. If you submit an answer without your name/student number, we will not be able to match it to you.

Your quiz grades will be available on Blackboard a week or two after each quiz. If you have any concerns about your grades or wish to review a quiz, please reach out to the tutors.


This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The hard copy bubble sheets will be submitted in the workshop (via collection by the tutors or course coordinator) each week at the end of the testing period for that week (in each class approximately 5 minutes after test completion in Weeks 2-9).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible. You can miss up to 2 quizzes and still earn full marks for this assessment. However, if you miss a quiz make sure you get the appropriate documentation at the time you miss the quiz, just in case you end up missing more than two and you need to provide it. If you miss more than 2 quizzes, then please contact the course coordinator to discuss your assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
27/07/2026 - 21/09/2026

The hard copy answer sheets will be submitted in the workshop (via collection by the tutors or course coordinator) each week at the end of the testing period for that week (each class in Weeks 2-9 at approximately 10 minutes before the scheduled end of class time).
assessments.task_description:
Each week you will be asked to write a short essay (up to 600 words) in the workshop. The essay question will be given in the last part of the workshop, and you will need to complete the test using pen and paper. This assessment will build from the activities completed in the workshop and challenge you to recall and apply what you have learned. The assessment given each week will only cover the course content (lectures, written material, class activities) for that week of content.

 

Please note:

  • You must be in the workshop for the whole time of the activities (so from a quarter past the hour when the class commenced) to be eligible to take the test that week. Essays can only be attempted in each workshop.
  • To take this test (i.e. write the essay for the week) you will need to produce a valid UQ student ID card. If you wish, you can bring a paper form dictionary with no annotation. This may be an English or dual language dictionary as required. No additional written, printed, or electronic material will be permitted. For the duration of the test, your mobile phones/smart watch must be turned off or in airplane mode and all other items must be placed on floor under your chair.
  • There are 8 tests overall (1 per episode for Episodes 1 to 8)
  • Each test is worth 10%
  • We will count your best 6 attempts at the tests
  • The tests contribute 60% towards your final percentage.
  • You must attempt the test for either Episode 7 or Episode 8 of the course in order to be awarded more than a Grade of 4 in the course. An attempt is defined as writing a minimum of 100 words on the worksheet test paper.
  • If you are unwell and unable to complete a test, you will only need to provide medical documentation in the event that you miss three or more tests. If you miss one or two tests, we will simply count your best six attempts at the other tests. If you miss a test however, make sure you get the appropriate documentation at the time you miss the test however, just in case you end up missing more than two and you need to provide it. If you miss more than 2 tests, you will need to supply documentation to cover all the dates you were absent from the workshops to be considered for supplementary assessment.
  • Important:  You must write your name/student number on your answer sheet for us to be able to give you the marks for this assessment item. If you submit an answer without your name/student number, we will not be able to match it to you based on your handwriting or pen colour.

 

Your essay grades will be available on Blackboard a week or two after each assessment. If you have any concerns about your grades or wish to review an assessment, please reach out to the tutors.

 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The hard copy answer sheets will be submitted in the workshop (via collection by the tutors or course coordinator) each week at the end of the testing period for that week (each class in Weeks 2-9 at approximately 10 minutes before the scheduled end of class time).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible. You can miss up to 2 tests (i.e., short essays written in class) and still earn full marks for this assessment. However, if you miss a test make sure you get the appropriate documentation at the time you miss the test, just in case you end up missing more than two and you need to provide it. If you miss more than 2 tests, then please contact the course coordinator to discuss your assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Episode 0: Introduction

Episode 0: Introduction (Workshop):  Please work through the Episode 0 materials (in the Episode 0 folder) on Blackboard before attending the workshop. It is essential that you attend the introductory workshop as we will be explaining the format of the course and the assessment requirements.You will have an opportunity in this class to practice the assessment format. These attempts are formative and will not count towards your grade.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02
